content: yq (mikefarah/yq v4) does not support --arg flag. Use env() pattern instead: name="$var" yq -r '.field == env(name)'. Three instances missed during original implementation.
context: run-eval.sh and cross-model-review.sh both used yq --arg which is jq-only syntax.

content: In POSIX sh, 'for var in $unquoted' word-splits on spaces. Convert to: tmpfile=$(mktemp); cmd > $tmpfile; while IFS= read -r var; do ...; done < $tmpfile. Do NOT pipe (printf | while read) when the loop body must modify outer-scope variables — pipes create subshells.
context: run-eval.sh had 7 for-in loops that would break on filenames with spaces.

content: In single-quoted grep patterns, a single backslash escapes the next char. '^\\.work/' matches literal backslash-dot, not dot. Use '^\.\.work/' for literal dot.
context: Refactored nested for loops to grep -Fxv, but double-escaped the dot in the .work/ filter.

content: For scripts with multiple temp files, use a temp directory with a single trap: _tmpdir=$(mktemp -d); trap 'rm -rf $_tmpdir' EXIT. Individual files go in $_tmpdir/name. No manual cleanup needed per-file.
context: run-eval.sh had 6+ temp files that would leak on early exit under set -eu.
